paper_title: Algorithmic Feature Highlighting for Human--AI Decision-Making

P000_S0033.reason: The surrounding context and related passages consistently define σ as a highlighting policy map, not an inverse; the sophisticated agent conditions on σ(X)=I, not σ⁻¹.

P001_S0036.reason: The surrounding context explicitly states the naive agent updates on 'the revealed values X_I' (not X_{-I}, the unrevealed values), and the naive posterior formula uses X_I.

P000_S0002.reason: The surrounding context explicitly states the naive agent 'ignores the informational content of which set I was selected' and 'treats the selection of I as exogenous,' contradicting conditioning on I.

P002_S0038.reason: The logical negation operator ¬ applied to a probability measure P is not well-formed; ¬ is a boolean/logical operator not applicable to probability distributions.

P001_S0005.reason: The surrounding context and related passages consistently describe the design problem as minimizing expected loss, not maximizing it; the perturbed max contradicts this.

P002_S0048.reason: The surrounding context defines Δ^N as the cost of deploying σ^S to naive agents, which must be R^N(σ^S) - R^N(σ^N) ≥ 0; the perturbed version reverses the subtraction, making it ≤ 0 by definition of σ^N being optimal.

P002_S0009.reason: The surrounding context states k=1 (at most one coordinate revealed), so revealing {1,2} violates the bandwidth constraint |I|≤k=1.

P000_S0010.reason: The surrounding context shows R^N(σ^S) = B²/8 + 1/8 and R^N(σ^N) = 1/4 = 2/8, so Δ^N = B²/8 + 1/8 - 2/8 = (B²-1)/8, not (B²+1)/8.

P001_S0014.reason: The surrounding context specifies a partition (C_1, C_2) into exactly 2 clusters corresponding to 2 signals, so summing to t=3 contradicts the 2-means problem being solved.

P002_S0019.reason: For a Bernoulli variable X_i with mean p_i, Var(X_i) = p_i(1-p_i), not p_i(1+p_i); the related passages confirm Var is used here.

P000_S0050.reason: The surrounding context shows p*_{d,i} equals p_{d,i} when p_{d,i}≤1/2 and 1-p_{d,i} otherwise, which is the minimum of the two values, not the maximum.

P001_S0053.reason: The preceding line gives β*·p²(1-p) + (1-β*)·p(1-p) = α·p(1-p) + (p-α)(1-p) since β*=α/p·p=α (not α·p²(1-p)); the perturbed form introduces an extra p factor inconsistent with the algebra.

P000_S0042.reason: The surrounding context explains this assumption means conditional means stay 'within ε of its unconditional mean,' which requires ≤ε, not ≥ε.

P002_S0059.reason: The surrounding context immediately after shows ᾱ(β) is defined as the integral from 0 to β of Q*(q)dq, and the convergence result uses ᾱ(β) not ᾱ(2β).
P002_S0059.original:
\begin{align*}
    &\E\bigl[\hat{\alpha}_d(\beta)\bigr]
    =
    \frac{1}{d}\sum_{j=1}^{d} \Ind(j\le \lfloor \beta d\rfloor)\: p^*_{d,(j)}
    \\
    &=
    \E\Bigl[\Ind\!\left(U\le \frac{\lfloor \beta d\rfloor}{d}\right)\,Q^*_d(U)\Bigr]
    =
    \E\bigl[\Ind(U\le \beta)\,Q^*_d(U)\bigr] + o(1)
    \\
    &\rightarrow
    \E\bigl[\Ind(U\le \beta)\,Q^*(U)\bigr]
    =
    \int_{0}^{\beta} Q^*(q)\,\de q
    =:
    \overline{\alpha}(\beta).
\end{align*}
P002_S0059.perturbed:
\begin{align*}
    &\E\bigl[\hat{\alpha}_d(\beta)\bigr]
    =
    \frac{1}{d}\sum_{j=1}^{d} \Ind(j\le \lfloor \beta d\rfloor)\: p^*_{d,(j)}
    \\
    &=
    \E\Bigl[\Ind\!\left(U\le \frac{\lfloor \beta d\rfloor}{d}\right)\,Q^*_d(U)\Bigr]
    =
    \E\bigl[\Ind(U\le \beta)\,Q^*_d(U)\bigr] + o(1)
    \\
    &\rightarrow
    \E\bigl[\Ind(U\le \beta)\,Q^*(U)\bigr]
    =
    \int_{0}^{\beta} Q^*(q)\,\de q
    =:
    \overline{\alpha}(2\beta).
\end{align*}
